instruction: Flag whether the comment is toxic.
baseline_prompt: You are a toxicity-moderation assistant. Flag whether the comment is toxic. Classify into one of these categories: not toxic, toxic.
source: SetFit/toxic_conversations via HuggingFace (see dataset card for license).

You keep changing your argument. You did this yesterday, citing the 43% who don't pay taxes as some sort of meaningful number, then said numbers are numbers. Today you said being paid $23.96 per hour as a Flagger in Lane County plus $13.82 "Fringe" was problem then faced with the job of flagger moved from that to local applications and anecdotal evidence in construction. It' like wack-a-mole. I do agree there are problems. But having just skimmed the wikipedia summary, I see more benefits that negatives. But my point (now) is that the issue is more complex than just being paid the prevailing wage which may be more than some contractors are willing to work. It comes down to values. As a taxpayer, for the most part I support Davis-Bacon, with reservations.

Sayfullo Saipov immigrated to the US legally in 2010. Canada vets immigrants through the same rigorous system of highly trained interviews, checks and balances as the US. Sayfullo Saipov was radicalized years after arriving in the US. So were others like him (i.e. Boston Marathon bombers). It simply is not safe to allow Muslims into our country regardless of how rigorous the vetting is. You cannot prevent them or their children from becoming radicalized by groups like ISIS after they become Canadian citizens. When a local Muslim immigrant gets radicalized and mows down your children with a UPS truck or rips a bomb off at a concert and kills your children in Canada then will you think differently? All nationalities, and all cultures are welcome in Canada, but I say stop it before it happens - NO to immigration of any kind for ALL Muslims from any country period. Enough is enough!

You have said before that your First Amendment rights are being violated by censorship in this format. As I have also said this is a privately owned space. If this space was public, you wouldn't have to pay to post. Taking it further, if we were really seeking free speech here, shouldn't this space be free to the public? Isn't this a kind of "free speech" gated community? You wrote some time ago that you post anonymously in the spirit of Benjamin Franklin. I suggest that if you wish to express your opinion uncensored, then write your own publication like Franklin did. He certainly was a good writer with many ideas. You too can become a Franklin in our times. If you do go this route, please let us know. I would like a copy. For me, Civil Comments is working pretty well. I like the egalitarian idea of reviewing each others comments. It's peer review and that is fine with me.

Eric Ryan 808 is right. The plan is for existing bus lines to be re-routed to the rail line. Many of those who ride the rail will not be new mass transit users, but existing bus riders. Furthermore, lots of those who now take one bus to get to where they want to go will have to take a bus to the rail line, take the rail, then get on another bus to go to their destination. The rail PR doesn't tell that side of the story.

Such shallow reasoning. There may have been secession even without slavery as an issue. There were many issues occurring. To preserve slavery all the southern states needed to do was remain IN the union and support the Lincoln backed Corwin admendment (original 13th admendment ). Modern revisionist history ignores what was written at the time and soon after the war. There war itself did have an underlying cause, money and power. When asked why not let the south go Lincoln replied where shall I get my revenue. The south provided some 80% off federal revenue in 1861 and Lincoln would not let it go. If you condider freedom self determination and the United States constitution positive ideas then one has to also consider the Confederate battle flag a positive symbol as it embodies all three.
